shortDescription.text: LDAP Injection via unsanitized user input in filter string

fullDescription.text: User-controlled input is directly interpolated into an LDAP filter string without escaping or validation. An attacker can manipulate the filter logic to bypass authentication, enumerate directory entries, or exfiltrate sensitive data from the LDAP directory.

shortDescription.text: LDAP query structure leaked in API response

fullDescription.text: The endpoint echoes back the constructed LDAP filter string and the raw username in the JSON response body, leaking internal directory query structure to the caller and aiding attacker reconnaissance.

shortDescription.text: No authentication required to query LDAP directory

fullDescription.text: The /api/ldap/search endpoint does not require any authentication token, session, or API key. Any unauthenticated client can perform LDAP queries against the directory, compounding the impact of the injection vulnerability.

message.text: LDAP injection in POST /api/ldap/search: the 'username' field from the request body is embedded directly into the LDAP filter '(&(objectClass=user)(cn={username}))' via f-string interpolation without escaping special characters. An attacker can supply a crafted value such as '*)(uid=*))(|(uid=*' to alter filter logic, bypass authentication checks, or dump arbitrary directory objects.

description.text: Escape all LDAP special characters in user input before interpolation using ldap.filter.escape_filter_chars() from python-ldap, or validate the username against a strict allowlist pattern (e.g., ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$) and reject any input that does not match.

message.text: The endpoint returns the full LDAP filter string and the raw username in the JSON response body. This reveals internal directory query structure to the caller and aids attacker reconnaissance for crafting injection payloads.

description.text: Remove 'ldap_query' and 'username' from the response body. Return only the search results required by the caller.

message.text: The /api/ldap/search endpoint has no authentication or authorization checks. Any unauthenticated client can submit arbitrary LDAP queries, which combined with the injection vulnerability allows unrestricted directory enumeration and data exfiltration.

description.text: Add authentication middleware (e.g., JWT token verification or session check) before processing the LDAP search request.
